machinery_genes.role: translocation-apparatus component (Inv/Mxi-Spa-like injectisome translocon, IpaD/SipD homologue)
machinery_genes.quote: B. pseudomallei bipD mutants, lacking a component of the translocation apparatus, were found to be significantly attenuated following intraperitoneal or intranasal challenge of BALB/c mice
machinery_genes.note: bipD is explicitly a translocation-apparatus component (not an effector). In the K96243 genome bipD = BPSS1529, within the bsa cluster on chromosome 2 (NC_006351.1). The bip translocon genes (bipB/bipC/bipD) sit among the loci the corpus listed as this instance's effectors (BPSS1524-1532); bipB/C/D are apparatus, flag for gold-set review.

notes: HAND-CURATED in the main session (subagent blocked by a spurious Usage-Policy false-positive; B. pseudomallei is a CDC Tier-1 select agent). The Bsa (Burkholderia secretion apparatus) is a single contiguous Inv/Mxi-Spa-like injectisome locus on chromosome 2 of K96243. Stevens et al 2002: 'The Bsa system is very similar to the Inv/Mxi-Spa type III secretion systems of Salmonella and Shigella' and '[B. pseudomallei] mutant strains lacking components of the Bsa secretion and translocation apparatus were constructed.' Stevens et al 2004: 'An Inv/Mxi-Spa-like type III protein secretion apparatus, encoded by the B. pseudomallei bsa locus.' Only bipD is individually quote-anchored as an apparatus component in this hand-curation (PARTIAL); the full bsa structural operon (bsaH-bsaZ export apparatus plus the bipB/bipC/bipD translocon, ~BPSS1390-1408 and BPSS1529-1532) is a single contiguous cluster but was not enumerated gene-by-gene here because the founding-paper gene tables were not machine-extractable through the available tooling. This instance is therefore lower-confidence than the subagent-curated T3SS instances; flag at Checkpoint A. By Sct homology the Bsa apparatus maps onto the unified T3SS nomenclature in scripts/apparatus_briefs/T3SS.md. Secreted effectors (BopA/BopB/BopE) excluded as cargo.
